message.content: {"schemaVersion":"0.1.0","decisionId":"pilot-e1-005-turn-1-player-0","agentId":"verifier-revision-llm","selectedActionId":"play-single-spade-3-copy1","teamObjective":{"type":"transfer_lead_to_partner","explanation":"Lead the cheapest non-trump single to preserve my modest high cards and give my partner an opportunity to take the trick with a higher card or a trump."},"partnerBelief":{"summary":"Partner's exact cards are unknown, but they might hold cards higher than 3 or some trumps; the hope is they can win the trick, though this is uncertain.","confidence":0.4,"evidence":[]},"opponentBelief":{"summary":"Opponents likely have cards higher than 3 and may have trumps, but no specific information about their exact holdings is available.","confidence":0.4,"evidence":[]},"actionRationale":{"primaryReason":"Playing the 3 is the cheapest lead. It preserves the 4, 5, and 6 for future tricks, does not commit any strength now, and lets the partner potentially take control without me asserting anything about hidden hands.","whyNotAlternatives":[{"actionId":"play-single-spade-4-copy1","reason":"The 4 is only slightly stronger and would waste a card that could later cover a 3 lead from another player."},{"actionId":"play-single-spade-5-copy1","reason":"Leading the 5 sacrifices a higher non-trump card while still being easily beaten by opponents or trump."},{"actionId":"play-single-spade-6-copy1","reason":"The 6 is my strongest non-trump; leading it now removes my best chance to win a later trick."}]},"riskAssessment":{"risks":["An opponent may easily beat the 3 and keep the lead.","The partner may not be able to win the trick, leaving our team on the defensive.","My remaining cards are still weak non-trumps."],"mitigation":"Retain the 4-5-6 for later tricks and avoid committing trumps or bombs at this early stage."},"confidence":0.7}

{"schemaVersion":"0.1.0","decisionId":"pilot-e1-012-turn-1-player-0","agentId":"verifier-revision-llm","selectedActionId":"play-single-diamond-3-copy1","teamObjective":{"type":"transfer_lead_to_partner","explanation":"Partner has only 2 cards left and the hand is tagged partner_near_finish, so the goal is to let partner win or cheaply handle the lead and possibly go out."},"partnerBelief":{"summary":"Partner likely has a compact hand and may be close to going out, but the exact ranks and suits of their remaining cards are unknown.","confidence":0.6,"evidence":["publicHistory:1c5e98c0-0f15-452f-b8a0-17e10364e58d","handCounts[2]","scenarioTags:partner_near_finish"]},"opponentBelief":{"summary":"Opponents hold the majority of the unseen cards; their exact strength is unknown at the opening lead.","confidence":0.3,"evidence":["publicHistory:1c5e98c0-0f15-452f-b8a0-17e10364e58d","playedCardSummary.playedCount:0","handCounts[1]:3","handCounts[3]:3"]},"actionRationale":{"primaryReason":"Leading the 3 of diamonds keeps my stronger cards (4-5-6) for later, gives partner the best chance to win or take the lead cheaply, and avoids revealing high-card strength this early.","whyNotAlternatives":[{"actionId":"play-single-diamond-4-copy1","reason":"Slightly stronger but still weakens the ability to play a low card later and does not materially help partner."},{"actionId":"play-single-diamond-5-copy1","reason":"Medium-high card is more likely to win the trick myself, leaving partner unable to lead and potentially stranded."},{"actionId":"play-single-diamond-6-copy1","reason":"Uses my strongest non-trump card on an opening lead where opponents are likely to have higher cards or trumps, wasting a useful later card."}]},"riskAssessment":{"risks":["Partner may not be able to beat a low 3 if their remaining cards are also low","An opponent may take the trick with a higher card or trump"],"mitigation":"By leading the lowest possible card I minimize resource expenditure and retain 4-5-6 for subsequent tricks or to regain the lead."},"confidence":0.75,"notes":"The verifier supplied only single-card plays; among them, the lowest card aligns with partner-support strategy on an opening lead."}

{"schemaVersion": "0.1.0", "decisionId": "pilot-e1-021-turn-2-player-1", "agentId": "verifier-revision-llm", "selectedActionId": "play-single-heart-4-copy1", "teamObjective": {"type": "gain_lead", "explanation": "Try to win the trick by beating the opponent's low lead with the cheapest possible card, so our team can lead next."}, "partnerBelief": {"summary": "Partner is unseen and may be able to beat a low lead, but no direct evidence about partner's exact cards is available.", "confidence": 0.5, "evidence": ["7f23ea6f-8877-4abf-88bf-6fcb7fe1ec05"]}, "opponentBelief": {"summary": "Player 0 likely led a low 3 of hearts to shed a card; the other opponents may hold higher cards that could beat a low follow.", "confidence": 0.6, "evidence": ["7f23ea6f-8877-4abf-88bf-6fcb7fe1ec05"]}, "actionRationale": {"primaryReason": "Play the 4 of hearts, the lowest card that beats the led 3. This contests the trick at minimal cost, keeping the 9 and 10 for later situations.", "whyNotAlternatives": [{"actionId": "pass", "reason": "Passing lets the leading opponent possibly win the trick cheaply and retain the lead."}, {"actionId": "play-single-heart-9-copy1", "reason": "The 9 also beats the 3 but spends a higher card unnecessarily when the 4 is sufficient."}, {"actionId": "play-single-heart-10-copy1", "reason": "The 10 is our highest card and risks wasting strength; the 4 provides the same contest at lower cost."}]}, "riskAssessment": {"risks": ["An opponent or partner may play a higher single and take the trick.", "If an opponent wins the trick, they may help the leading opponent exit later."], "mitigation": "Using the 4 preserves 9 and 10 for future tricks where high-card strength matters more."}, "confidence": 0.75}

{"schemaVersion": "0.1.0", "decisionId": "pilot-e1-024-turn-1-player-0", "agentId": "verifier-revision-llm", "selectedActionId": "play-single-spade-2-copy2", "teamObjective": {"type": "finish_hand", "explanation": "Player 0 holds only two non-trump singles and has the opening lead, so the immediate objective is to win the first trick if possible and then play the remaining card to finish the hand."}, "partnerBelief": {"summary": "Partner (player 2) holds three unknown cards. No specific ranks or suits are asserted; partner may be able to support later if they gain the lead, but this is uncertain.", "confidence": 0.4, "evidence": ["dd1471cb-0e15-4103-837d-3bd52f10ff4d"]}, "opponentBelief": {"summary": "Each opponent holds three unknown cards. They may hold trump 7s, jokers, or other cards that can beat a plain 2, but no hidden cards are asserted as facts.", "confidence": 0.4, "evidence": ["dd1471cb-0e15-4103-837d-3bd52f10ff4d"]}, "actionRationale": {"primaryReason": "Leading the higher plain 2 first maximizes the chance of winning the opening trick. If the 2 wins, player 0 regains the lead and plays the A next, which would empty the hand by the end of the following trick.", "whyNotAlternatives": [{"actionId": "play-single-spade-A-copy2", "reason": "Leading the weaker A first is more likely to be beaten immediately, leaving player 0 with only the 2 but without the lead. Recovering control from that position is uncertain, especially when opponents may lead combinations the remaining 2 cannot answer."}]}, "riskAssessment": {"risks": ["An opponent may hold a trump 7 or joker that beats the 2 and seizes the lead.", "Even if the 2 wins, the follow-up A could be beaten, though player 0 will still have played all cards."], "mitigation": "By leading the strongest single first, player 0 gives itself the best chance to control both remaining tricks and exit before opponents can exploit the weaker A."}, "confidence": 0.7, "notes": "Revised reasoning trace after verifier feedback: evidence references now point to the public new_game event, and hidden partner/opponent cards remain probabilistic."}

{"schemaVersion":"0.1.0","decisionId":"pilot-e1-026-turn-2-player-1","agentId":"verifier-revision-llm","selectedActionId":"play-single-heart-4-copy1","teamObjective":{"type":"suppress_opponent","explanation":"Player 0 (opponent) is down to 2 cards and led a low heart; beating this trick denies them continuity and reduces their chance to finish."},"partnerBelief":{"summary":"The public hand count shows partner (player 3) has three cards remaining, but their suit distribution and strength are unknown.","confidence":0.3,"evidence":[]},"opponentBelief":{"summary":"Player 0 may have only one card left after this lead and appears to be shedding a low heart, possibly to retain control or finish quickly.","confidence":0.6,"evidence":["985478fb-7d41-4d90-a038-461a35a76ffd"]},"actionRationale":{"primaryReason":"The 4 of hearts is the cheapest legal winner, taking the lead away from a near-finishing opponent while keeping the higher hearts (9, 10) for future tricks.","whyNotAlternatives":[{"actionId":"pass","reason":"Passing lets the opponent who has only 2 cards retain the lead, increasing the risk they finish their hand."},{"actionId":"play-single-heart-9-copy1","reason":"Beating with the 9 is unnecessary; it spends a higher card that may be needed later without improving the outcome over the 4."},{"actionId":"play-single-heart-10-copy1","reason":"Beating with the 10 wastes the highest heart and weakens future control over larger singles or the endgame."}]},"riskAssessment":{"risks":["Partner may have been able to beat the trick, so this play consumes a card the team might need later.","Winning the lead with limited high cards could expose me to opponent trumps or bombs."],"mitigation":"Use the minimum winning card (4 heart) to suppress the opponent while preserving the 9 and 10 for subsequent rounds."},"confidence":0.75,"notes":"Selected the lowest winning single to stop a near-finishing opponent with minimal resource use; beliefs are framed from public information and marked as uncertain."}

{"schemaVersion":"0.1.0","decisionId":"pilot-e1-029-turn-1-player-0","agentId":"verifier-revision-llm","selectedActionId":"play-single-spade-A-copy2","teamObjective":{"type":"finish_hand","explanation":"With only two cards while opponents hold three each, the priority is to play out both cards quickly. Leading the stronger of my two cards first maximizes the chance of winning the trick and keeping the lead for the final card."},"partnerBelief":{"summary":"Partner's exact cards are unknown; I have no public evidence about their holdings beyond what is visible to all players.","confidence":0,"evidence":[]},"opponentBelief":{"summary":"Opponents' exact cards are unknown; they may or may not hold cards that can beat my plays.","confidence":0,"evidence":[]},"actionRationale":{"primaryReason":"The Ace of spades is the higher of my two cards. If it wins the trick, I keep the lead and can play the 2 next. If an opponent can beat it, leading the 2 would likely also be beaten, so the Ace is at least as good as the alternative based only on the cards I can see.","whyNotAlternatives":[{"actionId":"play-single-spade-2-copy2","reason":"The 2 is the lower of my two cards. If opponents can beat the Ace, they can almost certainly beat the 2 as well, and leading low would give up the lead sooner."}]},"riskAssessment":{"risks":["An opponent may hold a trump or higher card that beats the Ace, causing me to lose the lead.","Even if the Ace wins, an opponent may win the next trick before I can play my final card."],"mitigation":"Leading the Ace first is the best available mitigation because it maximizes the chance of retaining control and playing the final card on the following trick."},"confidence":0.85,"notes":"Revised to avoid asserting hidden partner or opponent cards as facts; beliefs are expressed probabilistically and evidence is limited to public information."}

{"schemaVersion":"0.1.0","decisionId":"pilot-e1-034-turn-1-player-0","agentId":"verifier-revision-llm","selectedActionId":"play-single-spade-A-copy2","teamObjective":{"type":"finish_hand","explanation":"I hold only two cards while every other player holds three, so my top priority is to empty my hand quickly. Leading the higher single maximizes the chance of winning the trick, keeping the lead, and then playing the remaining low card to go out."},"partnerBelief":{"summary":"Partner has three hidden cards and may be able to support, but no specific cards are known.","confidence":0.3,"evidence":[]},"opponentBelief":{"summary":"Opponents collectively hold nine hidden cards. Some of those cards may be trumps or jokers that could beat a non-trump Ace, but this is not certain.","confidence":0.3,"evidence":[]},"actionRationale":{"primaryReason":"The Ace of spades is my strongest single. If it wins the trick, I will lead again with the 2 of spades and finish my hand. This keeps the initiative rather than surrendering it with a low card.","whyNotAlternatives":[{"actionId":"play-single-spade-2-copy2","reason":"Leading the 2 gives up the lead immediately and leaves the Ace stranded until I can regain the lead, which is uncertain and could slow down finishing the hand."}]},"riskAssessment":{"risks":["An opponent may have a trump or joker that beats the Ace, causing me to lose the lead with one card still in hand."],"mitigation":"Leading the Ace still maximizes the chance of winning the trick and going out on the next lead; leading the 2 would face the same or greater risk of never regaining the lead."},"confidence":0.75}

{"schemaVersion":"0.1.0","decisionId":"pilot-e1-042-turn-1-player-0","agentId":"verifier-revision-llm","selectedActionId":"play-single-diamond-3-copy1","teamObjective":{"type":"transfer_lead_to_partner","explanation":"Partner is down to 2 cards (near finish), so I want to lead a low single that partner may be able to win, giving them a chance to take the lead and go out. This is a probabilistic opportunity, not a guaranteed outcome."},"partnerBelief":{"summary":"Partner likely holds 2 cards and may be close to finishing, but I cannot assert their exact ranks or suits. I only know the public hand count.","confidence":0.5,"evidence":["4e0511e0-c31b-433a-9367-f146d105c092","handCounts"]},"opponentBelief":{"summary":"Opponents each hold 3 and 2 cards respectively; their exact compositions are unknown, and they may hold cards higher than mine or trump cards.","confidence":0.4,"evidence":["4e0511e0-c31b-433a-9367-f146d105c092","handCounts"]},"actionRationale":{"primaryReason":"Leading the lowest card (3 of diamonds) minimizes resource use, gives partner the best chance to win the trick and take the lead, and avoids wasting the slightly higher cards in my hand.","whyNotAlternatives":[{"actionId":"play-single-diamond-4-copy1","reason":"Slightly higher than the 3, but still very low and unlikely to win; playing it now wastes a card that is marginally stronger."},{"actionId":"play-single-diamond-5-copy1","reason":"A middle-low card that is unlikely to hold the trick; keeping it for later is better than leading it now."},{"actionId":"play-single-diamond-6-copy1","reason":"The highest card in my hand, but still non-trump and low; leading it risks losing my best card without a high chance of winning the trick."}]},"riskAssessment":{"risks":["An opponent may beat the low 3 with a higher card or trump and take the lead.","Partner may be unable to win the trick if their cards are even lower than the 3 or if opponents intervene."],"mitigation":"By leading the minimum card, I preserve the rest of my weak hand and do not commit resources; if the trick is lost, the loss is minimal."},"confidence":0.75}

{"schemaVersion": "0.1.0", "decisionId": "pilot-e1-043-turn-1-player-0", "agentId": "verifier-revision-llm", "selectedActionId": "play-single-club-3-copy1", "teamObjective": {"type": "minimize_loss", "explanation": "My hand contains only low non-trump clubs (3-6), and the public state shows an opponent is down to 2 cards. I cannot realistically expect to win the lead, so I dispose of my weakest card while limiting the value I surrender."}, "partnerBelief": {"summary": "I have no direct evidence about my partner's cards beyond the public new_game event; since my own hand is weak, my partner may by distribution hold some stronger cards, but this is speculative.", "confidence": 0.3, "evidence": ["42e5b43e-0077-47ec-b5fd-55052407183e"]}, "opponentBelief": {"summary": "The public scenario tag indicates an opponent is near finishing with only 2 cards remaining; they may hold cards strong enough to beat any of my low singles, but I cannot assert specific ranks or suits.", "confidence": 0.6, "evidence": ["42e5b43e-0077-47ec-b5fd-55052407183e"]}, "actionRationale": {"primaryReason": "Leading the 3♣ is the smallest card and follows the principle of disposing of low cards first when holding a weak hand. It minimizes the value surrendered if an opponent takes control.", "whyNotAlternatives": [{"actionId": "play-single-club-4-copy1", "reason": "Playing the 4♣ leaves a lower card stuck in hand and gives away slightly more value on a trick I am unlikely to win."}, {"actionId": "play-single-club-5-copy1", "reason": "The 5♣ is higher than necessary; retaining it preserves a slightly stronger card for later and avoids exposing extra value now."}, {"actionId": "play-single-club-6-copy1", "reason": "The 6♣ is my highest card; leading it wastes the most strength and leaves me with weaker follow-up cards."}]}, "riskAssessment": {"risks": ["The opponent with 2 cards may win the trick immediately and finish their hand", "My partner may not be able to cover if the trick comes back around"], "mitigation": "By leading the minimum card, I keep my slightly higher cards (4-6) for future turns and do not expose more value than necessary."}, "confidence": 0.7}

{"schemaVersion":"0.1.0","decisionId":"pilot-e1-045-turn-1-player-0","agentId":"verifier-revision-llm","selectedActionId":"play-single-spade-3-copy1","teamObjective":{"type":"keep_lead","explanation":"As the opening leader I want to start the trick cheaply. A low spade lead lets the trick develop without committing strength, and if my partner can cover it we retain control."},"partnerBelief":{"summary":"My partner has shown no cards, so their strength is unknown; they are somewhat likely to hold a card that can beat a low spade, but this is uncertain.","confidence":0.5,"evidence":[]},"opponentBelief":{"summary":"Opponents have shown no cards. Each opponent may hold higher spades, trumps, or nothing relevant, but I cannot assert any specific holding.","confidence":0.5,"evidence":[]},"actionRationale":{"primaryReason":"Leading the 3 of spades is the cheapest way to open the trick. It preserves my 4, 5, and 6 for later positions where they might beat lower opposing cards, and it gives my partner the widest range of cards that can win the trick without requiring them to spend high cards.","whyNotAlternatives":[{"actionId":"play-single-spade-4-copy1","reason":"The 4 is higher than needed for an opening lead and is more useful as a covering card later."},{"actionId":"play-single-spade-5-copy1","reason":"The 5 can already beat a 3 or 4; leading it wastes covering power and leaves me with weaker remaining cards."},{"actionId":"play-single-spade-6-copy1","reason":"The 6 is my highest non-trump card and my best chance to win or cover a trick later; it is too valuable to lead."}]},"riskAssessment":{"risks":["An opponent or partner may play a higher card or a trump and win the trick, so I may lose the lead immediately.","With only low spades and no trumps, regaining the lead later could be difficult if I lose it now."],"mitigation":"Leading the minimum card minimizes the value given away and keeps my slightly higher cards available for future tricks."},"confidence":0.75,"notes":"Opening lead from a very weak hand; the default is to lead the lowest available card and conserve covering cards."}

{"schemaVersion":"0.1.0","decisionId":"pilot-e1-047-turn-1-player-0","agentId":"verifier-revision-llm","selectedActionId":"play-single-diamond-3-copy1","teamObjective":{"type":"transfer_lead_to_partner","explanation":"Partner is down to 2 cards and the public scenario tag indicates they are near finish; leading the weakest single lets them take the lead if they can and avoids burning my only slightly higher cards."},"partnerBelief":{"summary":"Partner (player 2) is short-stacked with 2 cards and the public scenario tag partner_near_finish suggests they may be close to exiting; their exact cards are unknown.","confidence":0.3,"evidence":["scenarioTag:partner_near_finish","handCounts[2]=2"]},"opponentBelief":{"summary":"No information about opponent holdings; they have not played any cards.","confidence":0,"evidence":["publicHistory:8b411e0c-163e-4f2b-9ef3-e85ae0d8a868","playedCardSummary.playedCount=0"]},"actionRationale":{"primaryReason":"All four cards are weak non-trump diamonds. The lowest lead gives partner the best chance to win the trick and preserves my marginally higher diamonds for later.","whyNotAlternatives":[{"actionId":"play-single-diamond-4-copy1","reason":"Slightly stronger than the 3 but still weak; playing it now reduces a future higher option without meaningful upside."},{"actionId":"play-single-diamond-5-copy1","reason":"Middle card of a weak run; better kept for a later moment when card value matters more."},{"actionId":"play-single-diamond-6-copy1","reason":"The strongest card in hand; using it as an opening lead wastes the only card most likely to win against a low trick."}]},"riskAssessment":{"risks":["Partner may be unable to beat a low diamond, leaving opponents in control.","A 3 may still be beaten by any higher non-trump or trump."],"mitigation":"Accept the risk because the hand is weak; transferring initiative to a short-stacked partner is the highest-upside play."},"confidence":0.6,"notes":"No hidden partner or opponent cards are asserted as facts."}

{"schemaVersion":"0.1.0","decisionId":"pilot-e1-048-turn-1-player-0","agentId":"verifier-revision-llm","selectedActionId":"play-single-club-3-copy1","teamObjective":{"type":"transfer_lead_to_partner","explanation":"My hand is weak and contains only low non-trump clubs, so I want to play the smallest card to let my partner capture the trick and take control rather than keeping the lead myself."},"partnerBelief":{"summary":"Partner may hold higher non-trump or trump cards that can capture a low club lead, but no specific cards are known.","confidence":0.55,"evidence":["Public history only contains the new-game event (c147ec39-bbe5-4614-a2c7-2d7129bfe233), so partner's exact cards are unknown.","My private hand consists of low clubs only, making it likely that partner or opponents hold higher cards.","General game structure implies a partner can often cover a very low lead."]},"opponentBelief":{"summary":"Opponents likely hold higher cards than my clubs, and one opponent may be close to finishing, but exact holdings are unknown.","confidence":0.6,"evidence":["Public history only contains the new-game event (c147ec39-bbe5-4614-a2c7-2d7129bfe233), so opponents' exact cards are unknown.","My private hand has no trumps and only ranks 3-6 of clubs, so most unseen cards are probably higher.","The scenario tag opponent_near_finish is a public annotation that one opponent may have few cards remaining."]},"actionRationale":{"primaryReason":"Leading the 3 of clubs is the lowest legal play. It gives partner the best chance to capture the trick with a modest card, minimizes the value I expose, and preserves the 4-6 for later plays.","whyNotAlternatives":[{"actionId":"play-single-club-4-copy1","reason":"Still a low non-trump single, but it is a slightly more valuable card and does not improve the chance of transferring the lead compared with the 3."},{"actionId":"play-single-club-5-copy1","reason":"This consumes a middle card that could potentially beat a future low opponent lead; it is unnecessary when the 3 is available."},{"actionId":"play-single-club-6-copy1","reason":"Playing the highest club reduces my ability to respond to later low leads and is more likely to win the trick back to me with a weak hand."}]},"riskAssessment":{"risks":["An opponent may capture the trick with a higher card and retain the lead.","The opponent near finish could be in position to end the game soon if they gain the lead.","If all players pass, I win the trick and must lead again from a weak hand."],"mitigation":"Playing the lowest card makes it as cheap as possible for the opponents to take and keeps my remaining cards more flexible for subsequent turns."},"confidence":0.75}
